heading: National Aquaculture Development Plan
label: 2803 National Aquaculture Development Plan

children.text: Within eighteen months after , the Secretaries shall establish the National Aquaculture Development Plan.September 26, 1980

In developing the Plan, and revisions thereto under subsection (d), beginning not later than six months after , the Secretary shall consult with the Secretary of Commerce and the Secretary of the Interior, other appropriate Federal officers, States, regional fishery management councils established under , and representatives of the aquaculture industry. In addition, the Secretary shall give interested persons and organizations an opportunity to comment during the development of the Plan.September 26, 1980section 1852 of this title
identify aquatic species that the Secretaries determine to have significant potential for culturing on a commercial or other basis;

recommend actions to be taken by the public and private sectors (which may include, but are not limited to, research and development, technical assistance, demonstration, extension education, and training activities) that are necessary to achieve such potential;

children.label: A
children.text: aquaculture facility design and operation,

children.label: B
children.text: water quality management,

children.label: C
children.text: use of waste products (including thermal effluents),

children.label: D
children.text: nutrition and the development of economical feeds, including natural food sources,

children.label: E
children.text: life history, genetics, physiology, pathology, and disease control (including research regarding organisms that may not be harmful to fish and shellfish, but are injurious to humans),

children.label: F
children.text: processing and market development,

children.label: G
children.text: production management and quality control, and

children.label: H
children.text: the development of adequate supplies of seed stock;
include, where appropriate, research programs on the effect of aquaculture on estuarine and other water areas and on the management of such areas for aquaculture;
include, where appropriate, programs to analyze, and formulate proposed resolutions of, the legal or regulatory constraints that may affect aquaculture; and
include such other research and development, technical assistance, demonstration, extension education, and training programs as the Secretary deems necessary or appropriate to carry out this chapter.
with respect to those actions that the Secretary determines should be undertaken, the period of time within which each such action should be completed, in order to implement the Plan; and
with respect to each such action which of the Secretaries, acting individually, jointly, or collectively, has the responsibility for implementing the action.

children.label: A
children.text: the responsibilities conferred on the respective Secretaries by law or by any executive action having the effect of law (including, but not limited to, Reorganization Plan Numbered 4 of 1970);

children.label: B
children.text: the experience, expertise, and other appropriate resources that the department of each such Secretary may have with respect to the action required under the activity concerned; and

children.label: C
children.text: the concurrence of the Secretaries.
any aquatic species not currently identified in the Plan has significant potential for aquaculture;
any action specified in the Plan is not being accomplished on a successful and timely basis; or
any action specified in the Plan should be terminated because its objectives have been achieved or its projected benefits do not warrant further support;
a complete profile of the aquacultural industry with respect to the incidence, size, and status of commercial aquacultural enterprises;
the identification of the private and public institutions and organizations involved in aquacultural research, extension, credit, and market development;
the identification of the various aquatic species being cultured and a description of the status of commercial development of each of those species;
to the extent practicable, the identification of aquacultural production regions, species, and markets that have significant potential for development;
a catalog describing all Federal programs and activities that directly or indirectly encourage, support, or assist aquaculture; and
the identification of the economic, physical, legal, institutional, and social constraints that inhibit the development of aquaculture in the United States.
